reviews.review_msg: Paul has a sharp tongue sometimes, but he's a great lecturer and the subject matter in this course is very interesting. However, his feedback on essays leaves something to be desired. They often consist of a single word and/or offer very little useful critique.

Prof. Budra is one of those professors that EVERYONE should have at least once before they graduate. He cares about making the material engaging and is extremely well-respected in his field. So sad for SFU to hear that he's retiring next year, but so happy that he's had such a long and awesome career.
For someone who had no interest in Shakespeare prior to this class, Paul Budra made the learning experience enjoyable. Would recommend!

What's most difficult abt his classes is there's barely any assignments but a lot of reading. I don't mind how his courses are structured, he's a very engaging lecturer and loves bringing media/pop culture examples and refs into his old engl lectures. Hard to read the grading criteria but if you don't overthink it, you'll hit the right spot
great experience, want to take his class again

I am convinced that the 5 ratings is Budra doing this himself. There is no way this prof deserves this many 5s. I found him very condescending and him and his TAs move the goal post during the term and his teaching style is to basically just talk at you. Take Everton instead if you are at all able to.
remarkably unhelpful when I really needed his help. I have had many profs and he is amongst my worst experience at SFU

one of the best profs I've taken. he is such a funny guy and makes the tutorials and lectures enjoyable, he is super easy to approach and gives good feed back on assignment while also being a super quick marker. 100% suggest him as a prof! the one thing i will say is it is a bit difficult to keep up with the readings!

Paul is god sent. As someone who hates history, I was nervous to take an English focused on the past. Paul made everything EXTREMELY clear and I always left his lectures confident in what we were taught. He is funny too and definitely easy on the eyes

TAKE HIS CLASSES. He is my favourite prof; extremely funny, engaging, approachable, and discussion-oriented. He does a great job of making every class fun and thought-provoking. I took his gaming class as someone that NEVER games, and still found it extremely interesting and filled with opportunities for participation. He's super passionate.

Paul Budra is the best professor I have ever had the pleasure of learning from. His lectures are incredibly engaging and I believe he could make any subject interesting. He explains things very in depth, is hilarious and incredibly kind and approachable. He gave me and my friends free tickets to see his play! I adore him and highly recommend!

Dr. Budra is FIRE. You will need a lot of books because the course does require readings but they are decent and not too long. Dr. Budra explains the material well and is entertaining. The only thing that makes it difficult is the lack of assignments, but the final helps as a booster since it is open book. Overall, amazing class and professor.

Dr. Budra's interest in theatre is extremely visible throughout his lecture. He takes lots of time to clearly explain the material we cover in the course, and provides detailed feedback on papers. Dr. Budra made his lectures entertaining, and every easy to pay attention to. This is the first English class that I have truly been interested in.

honestly came into english thinking it was going to be tough, but Paul made it very easy to understand. final exam was easy as long as you understand the themes and core topics, and the final was open text. This is a reading and writing intensive course. so beware

Prof Budra is really passionate about theatre and it's definitely reflected in his lectures. Make sure to read the books or at least read sparknotes before lecture, it will make lectures have a lot more sense. Try to remember what he says about the meanings of the plays, it will help in final :)

Looking for a writing cred that won't bore you to death? Look no further! Prof. Budra's classes were short, sweet, informative and HILARIOUS. I cannot stress how funny and super sweet this guy is. he's super passionate abt his work and his theatrical background is apparent in his teaching. he doesn't post lec slide tho, so make a friend!
His lectures are really fun and engaging and his passion for theatre makes it even better. Answered any questions thoroughly and was always available through email/office hours.

Professor Budra is a carrying professor that is very passionate about his work. His love for theatre is what makes the class enjoyable. He genuinely wants you to succeed, and always makes sure his lectures are comprehensible and fun to attend. If his your professor, you're in good hands!!

One of the best teachers, professors, and role models at SFU. He is such a kind and caring individual who will take the time to talk to you about English. He is passionate and FUNNY; if you need a good laugh go take his class. Though there is lots of reading to do, he makes it easy to understand!
Professor Budra makes lectures engaging, blending his theater background to keep the class fun and easy to follow. Bring your books for the final, and use unstuck AI to prep - it's a lifesaver!

Good instructor with interesting lectures. Only thing I would say is that project feedback was not very in-depth. Got a B on a project but all the feedback was positive, so no idea how to improve the next grade. He is easily approachable though so could totally ask him in person.

He's funny during lecture and even if you're not into the content, he keeps you entertained. My issue is that there's no rubric. Like I have no idea how they were grading us, not to mention they mark hard with vague and unhelpful comments. Tutorials are also useless. Final is decent though, have a decent understanding of stuff and you'll be fine.
i hate english and i took this because i needed it but this lowkey had me loving theatre

Dr. Budra gave absolutely amazing lectures, his background in theater is obviously. Has the ability to cover alot of information in a short amount of time while keeping it easy to follow and fun. Would highly recommend his class. 10/10 would take again.

Paul Budra is excellent! I highly recommend him. I took English 211 with him and had a fabulous experience. Budra is the God of Renaissance/Early Modern literature, absolutely knowledgeable. Also, he always responds to emails super quickly. Take his class if you want a stress-free English class to enjoy the beauty of literature.
